policy_name: Coaching Schemes For SC/ST/OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) & Minority Students For Universities

sections.overview: Coaching schemes are implemented for SC, ST, OBC (Non-Creamy Layer), and minority students to provide equal opportunities in higher education. The schemes support remedial coaching, entry into services, and preparation for NET/SET exams, and are integrated with university development assistance.
sections.objective: The scheme aims to improve academic skills, enhance subject comprehension, prepare students for competitive exams and employment, and increase representation in higher education and teaching positions through NET/SET preparation.
eligibility: Universities and colleges under Section 2(f) and eligible under Section 12(B) of the UGC Act, 1956
Institutions with sufficient SC, ST, OBC (Non-Creamy Layer), and minority students
General candidates with BPL cards issued by Central or State Government can also apply
OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) and poor general candidates may be allowed up to 40% if sufficient target category students are not available
benefits: Financial assistance for coaching infrastructure and academic support
One-time grant up to Rs. 5 lakh for books, journals, AV aids, computers, photocopiers, generators or inverters
Annual recurring grant up to Rs. 7 lakh per scheme
Honorarium for coordinators and remuneration for teachers and scholars
Support for part-time staff and administrative costs
Special coaching for remedial education, competitive exams, and NET/SET preparation
Career guidance, mentoring, and counseling support
application_process: Contact the Registrar or Principal of the institution
Obtain and fill the prescribed application form
Submit the application to the concerned department
Ensure all details are correct before submission
After verification, eligible applicants can avail the benefits

faq.question: Who is eligible for coaching schemes for SC/ST/OBC (non-creamy layer) and minority students?
faq.answer: Students belonging to SC, ST, OBC (non-creamy layer), and minority communities are generally eligible. Criteria may also include academic qualifications, income, and valid certificates.

faq.question: What benefits do coaching schemes provide?
faq.answer: They provide training, expert faculty, study materials, mentorship, financial assistance, exposure to exam patterns, and personal development support.

faq.question: How can I apply for these coaching schemes?
faq.answer: Find the relevant scheme, check eligibility, collect documents, fill the application form, and submit it to the concerned authority within the deadline.

faq.question: Are there financial assistance or scholarships available?
faq.answer: Yes, many schemes provide financial support or subsidized coaching for economically disadvantaged students.

faq.question: How can I track my application status?
faq.answer: Use the reference or acknowledgment number provided after submission and follow updates from the concerned authority.

faq.question: Are coaching schemes only for competitive exams?
faq.answer: No, they are available for both competitive exams and university admission preparation.

faq.question: Where can I find more information?
faq.answer: Information is available on official government websites, educational institutions, and relevant authorities.
